Package in.co.rays.proj4.util

Class DataValidator

java.lang.Object
in.co.rays.proj4.util.DataValidator
public class DataValidator extends Object
Utility class to provide data validation rules across the application layer. Contains static methods to check numbers, formats, names, passwords, formats, and specific date types using Regular Expressions and type parsing handlers.
Version:
1.0
Author:
vinay

    Evaluates if a given date string can map safely to a concrete runtime Date context.

    • DataValidator

      public DataValidator()

  • Method Details

    • isNull

      public static boolean isNull(String val)
      Checks if the passed string value is null or has an empty trimmed length.
      Parameters:
      val - The string to check
      Returns:
      boolean true if null or empty, false otherwise

    • isNotNull

      public static boolean isNotNull(String val)
      Checks if the passed string value contains data (is neither null nor empty).
      Parameters:
      val - The string to check
      Returns:
      boolean true if containing data, false if null or empty

    • isInteger

      public static boolean isInteger(String val)
      Verifies whether the provided string can be safely parsed into a valid integer value.
      Parameters:
      val - The string to validate
      Returns:
      boolean true if it is a valid integer, false otherwise

    • isLong

      public static boolean isLong(String val)
      Verifies whether the provided string can be safely parsed into a valid long integer value.
      Parameters:
      val - The string to validate
      Returns:
      boolean true if it is a valid long integer, false otherwise

    • isEmail

      public static boolean isEmail(String val)
      Validates the email format criteria using a standardized regular expression.
      Parameters:
      val - The email address string to validate
      Returns:
      boolean true if it matches a valid email structural syntax pattern, false otherwise

    • isName

      public static boolean isName(String val)
      Checks if the provided text string matches standard structural conventions for human names. Excludes leading white spaces and includes typical letters or punctuation styles.
      Parameters:
      val - The name string to validate
      Returns:
      boolean true if matching structural specifications, false otherwise

    • isRollNo

      public static boolean isRollNo(String val)
      Validates whether the incoming string complies with structural Roll Number format requirements. Expects exactly two letters followed by three numeric digits.
      Parameters:
      val - The roll number string to validate
      Returns:
      boolean true if it strictly fits the tracking pattern requirement, false otherwise

    • isPassword

      public static boolean isPassword(String val)
      Validates password complexity against rigorous standard configuration criteria. Requires at least 1 digit, 1 lowercase letter, 1 uppercase letter, 1 special character, no whitespaces, and a total length between 8 to 12 characters.
      Parameters:
      val - The password string to validate
      Returns:
      boolean true if matching structural complexity parameters, false otherwise

    • isPasswordLength

      public static boolean isPasswordLength(String val)
      Simple standalone check to verify whether the password character bounds fall strictly between 8 and 12 characters.
      Parameters:
      val - The password string to validate
      Returns:
      boolean true if length constraints are met, false otherwise

    • isPhoneNo

      public static boolean isPhoneNo(String val)
      Validates standard mobile phone structural format constraints. Checks if the numbers are exactly 10 digits and start with index values of 6, 7, 8, or 9.
      Parameters:
      val - The phone number string to validate
      Returns:
      boolean true if structural criteria match, false otherwise

    • isPhoneLength

      public static boolean isPhoneLength(String val)
      Simple verification constraint tracking that checking value matches an explicit length of 10 digits.
      Parameters:
      val - The phone string to evaluate
      Returns:
      boolean true if exact count length requirement matches, false otherwise

    • isDate

      public static boolean isDate(String val)
      Evaluates if a given date string can map safely to a concrete runtime Date context. Uses internal parsing formats inside DataUtility.getDate(String).
      Parameters:
      val - The source formatted date expression string
      Returns:
      boolean true if structural parse handles cleanly, false otherwise

    • isSunday

      public static boolean isSunday(String val)
      Resolves whether the specific given calendar date value falls on a Sunday.
      Parameters:
      val - The target parsing date string expression
      Returns:
      boolean true if evaluated target represents Sunday, false otherwise

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      Execution entryway used for validating local rule conversion validation filters.
      Parameters:
      args - execution arguments block
